finding: Application submit success is a single small green line of text next to the button — no redirect, no form clear, no disable, no prominent confirmation.

evidence.quote: Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.
automation re-submitted 6 times because the success state was missed

persona_rationale: I move fast and look away the instant I click submit. A faint line of text beside the button is not a state change I'll register, so I'd assume it failed and resubmit or bounce — and there's already a /thank-you-for-applying page sitting unused.

suggested_fix.current: Inline green sentence beside SUBMIT APPLICATION button; form stays as-is
suggested_fix.proposed: Redirect to /thank-you-for-applying on success (or replace the form with a full-width confirmation card that clears/disables the form)

finding: No News/Blog item in the studio sidebar; /admin/news is reachable only by typing the URL.

evidence.quote: sidebar offers only SEO pages, Media library, and Settings
Media library : Images and source assets

persona_rationale: An admin creates news drafts repeatedly; forcing me to memorize a URL for a daily task is the kind of hidden-primary-action friction I won't tolerate in a tool I use often.

suggested_fix.current: Sidebar: SEO pages, Media library, Settings only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Blog & news' sidebar entry linking to /admin/news alongside SEO pages

finding: A 'Quick Tour · Step 1 of 3' overlay covers the outline panel on first editor open and blocks interaction until dismissed.

evidence.quote: QUICK TOUR · STEP 1 OF 3 / Page structure / This is where the structure of the page is.

persona_rationale: Forced tutorials are a hard trigger for me. The overlay points at the exact panel I need and stops me from working; even with a 'Skip tour' link I resent having to dismiss a tour before doing anything.

suggested_fix.current: Modal tour overlay auto-opens over the builder panel and blocks clicks
suggested_fix.proposed: Make the tour an opt-in '?' button; never auto-block the panel — or remember dismissal per-user so it shows at most once

finding: Hero stat counters render as '0+', '$0M+', '0+' placeholders.

evidence.quote:
0+
ENTREPRENEURS LAUNCHED
$0M+
SNACK/DRINK SALES IN VENDINGPRENEUR MACHINES
0+
VENDING LOCATIONS W/OUR GUIDANCE

persona_rationale: I judge legitimacy in 3 seconds. Zeroed-out vanity metrics read as broken or unconfigured data and make the whole site feel unmaintained.

suggested_fix.proposed: Populate real figures, or remove the stat band until real numbers exist — never ship placeholder zeros

finding: Contact link exists only in the footer; it is not in the header/primary navigation.

evidence.quote: link is in the FOOTER only — CONTACT US; not in the header nav
navigation "Primary": link "About" link "Resources" link "Case Studies" link "News"

persona_rationale: My reflex is the top nav. When Contact isn't there I waste time scrolling to the footer or guessing the URL — avoidable hunting.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add 'Contact' to the primary header navigation

finding: The news listing page is headed 'BLOG' while the nav item and route are both 'NEWS'.

evidence.quote: BLOG heading on /news page
NEWS

persona_rationale: Two names for the same section makes me second-guess whether I'm in the right place. Inconsistent labels slow down the mental model I build to navigate fast.

suggested_fix.proposed: Use one term everywhere — change the page heading to 'NEWS' to match nav and route

finding: Article cards are scroll-reveal animated and render as blank outlines above the fold until scrolled into view.

evidence.quote: empty card outlines before any scrolling

persona_rationale: Empty outlines on first paint look like a half-loaded or broken page; my 3-second judgment is 'this isn't ready' before I even scroll.

suggested_fix.proposed: Render cards visible by default; apply reveal animation only to below-fold cards, or drop reveal entirely on the listing

finding: No autofocus on the first form field; the page loads with no field focused.

evidence.quote: Name field empty and unfocused on load

persona_rationale: I want to land and start typing. Having to click into the Name field first is one extra interaction on every single form.

suggested_fix.proposed: autofocus the Name input on /apply (and the first field on /contact)

finding: Phone field is a plain text input rather than type=tel, so it offers no numeric keypad and no input affordance.

evidence.quote:
text: Phone
textbox "Phone"

persona_rationale: Wrong input types are sloppy and slow me down — on any device I expect the phone field to behave like a phone field.

suggested_fix.proposed: <input type="tel" inputmode="tel"> for Phone on /apply and /contact

finding: Contact Phone field is also a plain text input, not type=tel.

evidence.quote:
text: Phone
textbox "Phone"

persona_rationale: Same wrong-input-type friction repeated on the contact form — consistency in sloppiness, not in quality.

suggested_fix.proposed: <input type="tel" inputmode="tel"> for the contact Phone field

finding: No keyboard submit (Cmd/Ctrl+Enter) on the application form; submitting requires tabbing to or clicking the button.

evidence.quote: /apply tabOrder ... 16 button 'SUBMIT APPLICATION'

persona_rationale: Keyboard-first users expect Cmd+Enter to submit a focused form. Reaching for the mouse or tabbing to the button at the end is needless friction.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add Cmd/Ctrl+Enter keyboard submit handler to the apply and contact forms

finding: State is a 50-option native <select> with no typeahead-by-name UX beyond default browser behavior; budget/stage/timeline are all 'Select' placeholder dropdowns with no smart default.

evidence.quote: combobox "State": option "Select" [disabled] [selected] option "Alabama" ...

persona_rationale: Four 'Select' dropdowns with no pre-selection means four manual picks. A combobox with type-to-filter (or geo-prefilled State) would cut the form time noticeably.

suggested_fix.proposed: Use a searchable combobox for State (type 'TX' to jump); consider geo-prefilling State from IP as an editable default

finding: Homepage has a measured layout shift of CLS 0.263 and horizontal scroll on mobile.

evidence.quote: Layout shift (CLS): 0.263
mobile-375 ... horizontal scroll present

persona_rationale: Visible jumping during load and a page that scrolls sideways on mobile both read as unpolished — I notice jank immediately and it lowers my confidence in the build.

suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve dimensions for hero/image/logo-strip to bring CLS under 0.1; fix the element causing horizontal overflow at 375px width

finding: A news hero image fails to load (500 from the Webflow CDN via next/image), showing alt text where the image should be.

evidence.quote: 500 /_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F...png&w=640&q=75
third card shows 'Top 10 Profitable Products...' alt text instead of an image

persona_rationale: A broken image on the first content card is the kind of visible defect that makes a developer-minded user assume the rest is fragile too.

suggested_fix.current: next/image proxying a remote Webflow CDN URL that 500s
suggested_fix.proposed: Self-host the image or fix the remote URL/loader config; add a graceful image fallback so a failed load doesn't expose raw alt text

finding: Creating an SEO page is a 3-step wizard (Page type -> Starting point -> Ready to build) before the editor opens.

evidence.quote: 3-step wizard opens (Page type -> Starting point -> Ready to build)

persona_rationale: Three steps to start a page is ceremony. After I've made one, I know what I want — I'd rather drop straight into the editor with sensible defaults than click through a wizard each time.

suggested_fix.current: Mandatory 3-step wizard on every Create page
suggested_fix.proposed: Default 'Create page' to SEO/Resource + blank, opening the editor directly; keep the wizard behind a 'Choose a template' secondary option

finding: Publish is gated behind readiness warnings ('Fix SEO title', 'Fix URL slug') with no inline jump-to-field link.

evidence.quote: PUBLISH STATUS draft ... Fix URL slug

persona_rationale: The gating is reasonable, but as a power user I want the warning to jump me straight to the offending field, not just tell me to fix it.

suggested_fix.current: Static 'Fix SEO title / Fix URL slug' text
suggested_fix.proposed: Make each readiness warning a link that focuses/scrolls to the relevant field

finding: The redirects manager has zero inbound links from any explored admin page; it is reachable only by URL.

evidence.quote: Back to pages
Redirects | NO PATH FOUND — /admin/pages/redirects is not linked from any explored admin page

persona_rationale: Redirects are exactly the kind of thing a power user reaches for often; an orphaned URL-only page means I have to bookmark it or remember the path.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Redirects' link in the SEO pages page header or sidebar

finding: The same content type is labeled inconsistently: 'News' (route), 'Blog CMS'/'New blog post' (editor), 'NEWS' (public nav), 'BLOG' (public heading).

evidence.quote: page is headed 'New blog post' under breadcrumb 'Blog CMS'
Blog and news

persona_rationale: Four names for one thing forces me to keep re-mapping terms in my head — friction every time I navigate the admin.

suggested_fix.current: News / Blog CMS / New blog post / NEWS / BLOG used interchangeably
suggested_fix.proposed: Standardize on one term (e.g. 'Blog') across admin breadcrumbs, public nav, and public heading

finding: News list rows have no delete/archive affordance; retiring a post requires opening each post's editor to find the Archive control.

evidence.quote: no delete control in the list row; the EDITOR has an Archive button

persona_rationale: Bulk-managing content from the list is a core power-user expectation. Having to open every post to archive it is several extra clicks per item.

suggested_fix.current: Archive only inside each post editor
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a per-row actions menu (Edit / Archive / Delete) on the news list, matching the SEO pages list pattern

finding: SEO pages list row actions menu did not expose Archive/Delete to keyboard/automation, and the editor exposes no archive control either — page retirement path is unclear despite 2 archived pages existing.

evidence.quote: row '⋮' actions menu did not expose Archive/Delete items to automation
the builder editor offers no archive/delete path that automation could find

persona_rationale: If I can't reliably find how to retire a page, I lose confidence I can clean up my own mistakes quickly — and cleanup speed matters to me.

suggested_fix.current: Archive/Delete hard to reach from row menu and absent from editor
suggested_fix.proposed: Ensure the row '⋮' menu reliably exposes Archive/Delete and keyboard-opens; add an Archive control in the page editor

finding: Each video testimonial occupies three consecutive keyboard tab stops on the homepage, inflating the tab sequence.

evidence.quote: / tabOrder stops 9-11 all 'video' 'Video testimonial from Thomas Rohlader' (repeated x3 per testimonial)

persona_rationale: As a keyboard user, tabbing through the homepage means three stops per testimonial card — a long, repetitive sequence to get past the proof section to a CTA.

suggested_fix.proposed: Collapse each video to a single focusable control; remove redundant focusable wrappers

finding: Homepage stat values (dd elements) fail WCAG AA color contrast.

evidence.quote: color-contrast (serious) — .grid-cols-[auto_1fr].items-baseline.py-5 > dd, count 3

persona_rationale: Low-contrast metric labels are hard to scan quickly; combined with the '0+' placeholders, the whole stat band is weak.

suggested_fix.proposed: Darken/strengthen the stat value text color to meet 4.5:1

finding: Legacy /blog/* article path 404s instead of redirecting to the /news/* equivalent.

evidence.quote: 404 /blog/how-to-choose-the-perfect-location-for-vending-machine

persona_rationale: Given the BLOG/NEWS naming confusion, I might type or follow a /blog/ link and hit a dead 404 — a redirect would catch that bounce.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 301 redirect from /blog/{slug} to /news/{slug}

finding: The Settings sidebar link deep-links straight to /admin/settings/users; /admin/settings itself only resolves by redirect, so there's no settings landing/index.

evidence.quote: Settings ... deep-links to /admin/settings/users; /admin/settings itself is only reachable by URL

persona_rationale: If settings ever grows beyond users, the lack of a settings index means I can't see the full set of settings sections at a glance.

suggested_fix.proposed: Keep the redirect for now but plan a settings index/landing once a second settings section exists
